title: EEG: RL Task (3-Armed Bandit) with alcohol cues in hazardous drinkers and ctls
description: This dataset comprises electroencephalography (EEG) recordings from 53 community participants performing a three-armed bandit reinforcement learning task with alcohol and beverage cues. The study compares neural responses between hazardous drinkers and controls, with data collected between 2019-2021 at the University of New Mexico's Clinical Research Center Lab. The dataset aims to identify electrophysiological markers of aberrant cue-specific exploration patterns associated with hazardous alcohol use.

funding_references.funder_name: National Institute on Alcohol Abuse and Alcoholism
National Institute on Alcohol Abuse and Alcoholism

methods_description: EEG data were collected from 53 participants performing a three-armed bandit reinforcement learning task with alcohol versus beverage cues. Data collection occurred between 2019-2021 at the Clinical Research Center Lab (CRCL) at the University of New Mexico.
